title: HilbertBench Span
description: A causal execution unit containing top-level physical evidence and an immutable sequence of execution-boundary events.

parent_span_id.description: Causal parent span. Null if root.

sequence_number.description: Authoritative monotonic index representing strict causal ordering within the trace. Guaranteed by the recorder.

timestamp_start.description: UTC nanoseconds when the span was initialized.

backend_id.description: Execution target (hardware/simulator). Null if not applicable.

payload_ref.description: SHA-256 reference to the primary execution input (circuit, pulse schedule). Promoted to top-level for columnar query performance.

outcome_ref.description: SHA-256 reference to execution result (counts, memory). Null if failed or in-flight. Promoted to top-level.

inline_artifacts.description: Small, per-span artifacts embedded directly in the span record to avoid per-artifact file I/O. Keys are 'sha256:<hex>' content hashes; values are the artifact data and metadata. Use for outcomes, parameters, and observables. Structural artifacts (circuits, calibration) remain in the file store.

event_type.description: Namespaced event identifier (e.g., EXECUTION_REQUEST, PENNYLANE_GRADIENT_STEP). Enum intentionally omitted to allow extension.

timestamp.description: UTC nanoseconds for this specific point-in-time event.

error_ref.description: SHA-256 reference to an error artifact (e.g., full traceback), if applicable.

attributes.description: POLICY: Must only contain scalar, non-reproducibility-critical metadata (e.g., queue position, API latency). Physical math and arrays MUST go in payload_ref/outcome_ref.

inline_artifact.description: An artifact whose content is embedded directly in the span record rather than written to a separate file. The sha256 hash of the data string is the key in inline_artifacts.

kind.description: Semantic type. Only per-span, non-structural kinds are valid inline. Circuits and calibration snapshots must remain in the file store.

encoding.description: Serialization format of the data field. Always text-safe for JSONL embedding.

data.description: The artifact content, serialized as a UTF-8 string. For 'json' encoding: a JSON-encoded value (number, array, or object). For 'plaintext': raw text.

size_bytes.description: Byte length of the data string. Allows fast size checks without re-serializing.

created_at.description: UTC nanoseconds when this artifact was recorded.
